content: I'm trying to see If I can use a RUST for a new kernel module I'm planning to write. I'll have to use the following Kernel APIs kthread_create_on_node(), init_completion(), smp_call_function_single() and related functions. Any views on if that's possible?
A message was moved here from #Modules > E1000 by Boqun Feng.

• For kthread_create_on_node(): I have a WIP for thread creation support: https://github.com/fbq/linux/commit/6d146afc8f66c9fdebf6aade01a4ace2abd9a621 , but you should check whether you can use workqueue to replace the thread usage if you are going to upstream the driver, because compared to a kernel thread, using workqueue is better and cheaper.

  • For init_completion(): please see if CondVar works for you.

  • For smp_call_function_single(): there is an old PR: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/851 from @Jon Olson
BTW, I move your original question to a dedicated topic, feel free to rename it.
